summary: FiveM game cheat with disguised persistence, hardware fingerprint exfiltration, and obfuscated remote payload
details: Package advertises itself as 'Sensivity Control Panel' but ships a Windows-only FiveM/GTA V game cheat. launcher.js loads a 6.6MB native module sens.node containing memory-hook strings ('Freecam', 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot/LeftHand/Head', 'License Accepted') and locates target game processes via PowerShell. On invocation, launcher.js reads the 175KB obfuscator.io-output server.obf.js (rotated string array, RC4-style decoder, control-flow flattening) and passes it to eval(); the obfuscated code POSTs a hardware fingerprint — Windows MachineGuid (read from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid via PowerShell), os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial — as JSON to a hardcoded LICENSE_URL (`https://...opi.com/ch...`). ensureAutostart() installs HKCU\...\Run\OneDriveUpdate pointing to a shipped OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs wrapper that re-launches the obfuscated payload at every login — both the registry value name and the VBS filename impersonate Microsoft OneDrive's updater. Both supervisor and worker processes set process.title to 'Runtime Broker' to masquerade as a Windows system process in Task Manager. checkYouTube() writes a temporary PowerShell script that uses UIAutomation to scrape address bars from Chrome/Edge/Opera/Opera GX/Brave looking for a specific YouTube video ID; the cheat UI is gated on whether the operator has that exact video open. Combined: obfuscated remote-driven payload + native game-cheat binary + impersonated-Microsoft persistence + process-name masquerade + browser surveillance + host-fingerprint exfiltration to a hardcoded third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
